        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/i18n/utils.ts",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 8,

            "line": 23,

        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "// This function is used in the starter blog to get the translations",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "export function useTranslations(lang: keyof typeof ui = defaultLang) {",
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "  return function t(key: keyof (typeof ui)[typeof defaultLang], tags?: Record&lt;string, string&gt;) {",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "    const translation = ui[lang][key] || ui[defaultLang][key];",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "    // If the translation is not found, return the key",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (!translation) return key;",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "    // Replace the tags in the translation with the values passed, if any",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "    // example: t('components.themeSwitcher.toggleDarkMode', { darkMode: 'dark mode' })",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "    // where in the translation file we have: 'Toggle {darkMode}'",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "    return (ui[lang][key] || ui[defaultLang][key]).replace(/\\{(\\w+)\\}/g, (_, match) =&gt; tags?.[match] || \"\");",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "  };",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "isSignature": true
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.20046889781951904
    },

      "codeBlock": {

          {
            "text": "export const ui = {",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "  'en': {",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "    // Site Metadata",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./README.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 40
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 75
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "- Projects page",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "- i18n support",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Fully SEO optimized**, including Open Graph and Twitter Cards",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "This site is built on the [tailwind-astro-starting-blog](https://github.com/wanoo21/tailwind-astro-starting-blog) template. Notable features include:",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "- Support for multiple authors pages - **each author will have** its own page, with a list of all the posts written by him/her.",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "- Support for multiple tags pages - **each tag has its own page with custom description**, that can be customized with markdown.",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "- Related posts - **each post has** a list of related posts, provided by the author inside the markdown file.",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "- Pagination inside blog/tags pages - **each blog/tags page has** a pagination.",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "- Cool animations provided by Astro.",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "## Internationalization",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "This template has support for i18n, it's set up for English as default. You can add more languages in the &#96;src/i18n/ui.ts&#96; file. It doesn't have support for dynamic language change, but it's easy to implement.",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "## Extend / Customize",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;src/consts.ts&#96; contains a list of constants that you can customize to your liking, including the blog title, description, author, social media links, and open source projects (&#96;OPEN_SOURCE_PROJECTS&#96;), etc.",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;src/functions.ts&#96; contains a list of functions that changes the default behavior of the template, including default post sorting and exclude draft posts.",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;src/content/authors/default.mdx&#96; contains the default author information. You can add more authors by adding more &#96;.mdx&#96; files in the &#96;src/content/authors&#96; folder.",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;src/content/tags/default.mdx&#96; contains the default tag information. You can add more tags by adding more &#96;.mdx&#96; files in the &#96;src/content/tags&#96; folder.",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;src/content/config.ts&#96; contains all fields for author, blog and tags pages. Check what's required and what's not. You can also add more fields if you want to.",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "## Comments",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "This template now supports [Giscus](https://giscus.app/) comments, which uses GitHub Discussions as a backend. To enable comments on your blog:",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "1. **Enable GitHub Discussions** on your repository:",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "   - Go to your repository settings",
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "   - Scroll down to the \"Features\" section",
            "lineNumber": 75
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.17868280410766602
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/content/authors/default.mdx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 27
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "name: Ion Prodan",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "avatar: /src/assets/body.png",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "occupation: Frontend Engineer",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "shortBio: Ion Prodan is a frontend engineer building open-source tools, email editors, and developer-focused products on the web.",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "company: ngcomma.io",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "twitter: https://twitter.com/ipwanciu",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "linkedin: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ion-prodan",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "github: https://github.com/wanoo21",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "techStack:",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "  - Angular",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "  - TypeScript",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "  - Astro",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "  - Node.js",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "  - TailwindCSS",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "  - MJML",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "  - Web Components",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "I'm a frontend engineer with a passion for building things on the open web. I work mostly with **Angular**, **TypeScript**, and **Astro**, and I care a lot about developer experience, performance, and making useful tools that other developers actually enjoy using.",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "Over the years I've shipped [ngb.email](https://github.com/wanoo21/ngb.email) — a fully customizable, open-source drag-and-drop email template editor for Angular, integrated with MJML and TailwindCSS (no longer maintained) — [tailwind-astro-starting-blog](https://github.com/wanoo21/tailwind-astro-starting-blog), a popular Astro + Tailwind blog starter — [snipsco.com](https://snipsco.com), code snippets made easy, built entirely with Angular — and [quiky.email](https://quiky.email), free AI-powered email writing tools built with Astro.",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "I find the intersection of **tooling**, **developer productivity**, and **great UX** most interesting. When I'm not building, I'm thinking about how to improve the tools I already have.",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "This blog is where I write about things I learn, build, and think about. Mostly frontend, sometimes architecture, occasionally things that just won't fit in a tweet.",
            "lineNumber": 26
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.17678290605545044
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/components/solidjs/ThemeSwitcher.tsx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 63
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 48,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "import { SITE_METADATA } from \"@/consts\";",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "import { useTranslations } from \"@/i18n\";",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "const t = useTranslations();",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "export default function ThemeSwitcher() {",
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "  const mediaQuery = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)');",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "  let preferredTheme = mediaQuery.matches ? 'dark' : 'light';",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "  if (SITE_METADATA.theme !== 'system') {",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "    preferredTheme = SITE_METADATA.theme;",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "  }",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "  const savedTheme = localStorage.getItem('theme');",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [theme, setTheme] = createSignal(savedTheme || preferredTheme);",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "  onMount(() =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "    mediaQuery.addEventListener('change', (event: MediaQueryListEvent) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "      if (!savedTheme) {",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "        setTheme(event.matches ? 'dark' : 'light');",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "      }",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "    });",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "  });",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "  createEffect(() =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "    document.documentElement.classList.remove(theme() === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark');",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "    document.documentElement.classList.add(theme());",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "    localStorage.setItem('theme', theme());",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {

          {
            "text": "export const SITE_METADATA = {",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/components/solidjs/SearchButton.tsx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 10,
            "column": 1
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 233,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "export default function SearchButton() {",
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = createSignal(false);",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [query, setQuery] = createSignal(\"\");",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [results, setResults] = createSignal&lt;SearchResult[]&gt;([]);",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [allPosts, setAllPosts] = createSignal&lt;SearchResult[]&gt;([]);",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = createSignal(false);",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "  // Load search data when component mounts",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "  createEffect(() =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (isOpen() &amp;&amp; allPosts().length === 0) {",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "      setIsLoading(true);",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "      fetch(\"/search.json\")",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "        .then((res) =&gt; res.json())",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "        .then((data) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "          setAllPosts(data);",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "          setIsLoading(false);",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "        })",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "        .catch((error) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "          console.error(\"Error loading search data:\", error);",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "          setIsLoading(false);",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "        });",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "  });",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "  // Perform search when query changes (with debouncing)",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "  createEffect((prevTimeout?: NodeJS.Timeout) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "    const searchQuery = query().toLowerCase().trim();",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "    // Clear previous timeout before setting up new one",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (prevTimeout !== undefined) {",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "      clearTimeout(prevTimeout);",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (!searchQuery) {",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "      setResults([]);",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "      return undefined;",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "text": "    // Debounce search by 150ms",
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "    const timeout = setTimeout(() =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "      const filtered = allPosts().filter((post) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "        const titleMatch = post.title.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery);",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "        const summaryMatch = post.summary.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery);",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "        const contentMatch = post.content.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery);",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "        const tagsMatch = post.tags.some((tag) =&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "          tag.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery)",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "        );",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "        return titleMatch || summaryMatch || contentMatch || tagsMatch;",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "      });",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "      setResults(filtered);",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "    }, 150);",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "text": "    // Return timeout to be passed as prevTimeout in next run",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "    return timeout;",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "  });",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "  // Handle keyboard shortcuts",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "  onMount",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 234,
            "isSignature": true
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.16656990349292755

          {
            "text": "---",

          {
            "text": "import \"@/styles/global.css\";",

            "lineNumber": 27
          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "// The default author is the site creator",

          },
          {
            "text": "const creator = await getEntry(\"authors\", \"default\");",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/content/blog/snipsco.mdx",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 20
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 47
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "They are beautiful, super fast, and I still respect them a lot. But they only give you one fixed code window. Nothing more. No arrows, no written explanations, no shapes around important parts, no moving blocks freely. They are perfect for simple shares, but not enough when you want to teach, explain, or make your code look really professional and clear.",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "_So I was looking for something more than that!_",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "There was no plan for me to build something like Snipsco. But one day, on a whim, I asked Gemini Pro on [Google Studio](https://aistudio.google.com) to create a prototype using Angular, and surprisingly, it did a good job.",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "**That was the time when my (it might be quite challenging) had broken!** In a matter of a second, I understood I can make it, and make it so much better that everyone will want to use it, simply everyone!",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "From that moment, everything went fast. I took the Gemini prototype, migrated it to a real Angular application, rewrote many parts, fixed bugs, made the canvas smooth with snapping, added gradients, Prism highlighting, Prettier formatting, layers, rotate, resize - everything I needed.",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "Got the idea for the domain using [Vercel domains](https://vercel.com/domains) (a very nice tool) and bought it on Cloudflare. ",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "That's how fast [Snipsco](https://snipsco.com/) was born.",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "I MUST mention here that AI made me code faster than EVER, I need to mention it again: **FASTER THAN EVER!** No joke!",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "## Where Snipsco is right now",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "Right now, Snipsco is a working preview. You paste your code, it gets formatted, you open the sidebar and add shapes, arrows, text, and images. You drag, change themes, gradients, and colors. Export PNG or to the clipboard.",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "It is still free for everyone, and the workspace will remain free forever, with no limits. **Use it to share beautiful code, teach others - this is the most important part for me.**",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "But of course, this is just the beginning.",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "## Where Snipsco is heading",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "Snipsco's route is a long, long path, and of course, it includes AI!",
            "lineNumber": 46
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.16448765993118286
    },

            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "const { title, description, imgSrc, href } = Astro.props;",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },

            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;div class=\"md max-w-[544px] p-4 md:w-1/2\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 18

            "text": "  &lt;div",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },

            "text": "  &gt;",

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 25
          },

            "text": "            &lt;Image",

          },

            "lineNumber": 33

            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "            alt={title}",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {

          },
          {

          },

          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "import Card from \"@/components/Card.astro\";",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "import { useTranslations } from \"@/i18n\";",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "import RootLayout from \"@/layouts/RootLayout.astro\";",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "const t = useTranslations();",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "const projectsData = [",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "  {",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "    title: \"A Search Engine\",",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "    description: &#96;What if you could look up any information in the world? Webpages, images, videos",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "    and more. Google has many features to help you find exactly what you're looking",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "    for.&#96;,",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "    imgSrc:",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 15
          },

            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "  },",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "  {",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "    title: \"The Time Machine\",",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "    description: &#96;Imagine being able to travel back in time or to the future. Simple turn the knob",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "    to the desired date and press \"Go\". No more worrying about lost keys or",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "    forgotten headphones with this simple yet affordable solution.&#96;,",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "    imgSrc: \"https://placeholder.com/500x190\",",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "    href: \"/blog/the-time-machine\",",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "  },",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "];",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "&lt;RootLayout",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "  title={t(\"pages.projects.title\")}",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "  description={t(\"pages.projects.description\")}",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "  &lt;div class=\"divide-y divide-gray-200 dark:divide-gray-700\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "    &lt;div class=\"space-y-2 pb-8 pt-6 md:space-y-5\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "      &lt;h1",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "        class=\"text-3xl font-extrabold leading-9 tracking-tight text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-100 sm:text-4xl sm:leading-10 md:text-6xl md:leading-14\"",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },

          },
          {
            "text": "        {t(\"pages.projects.allProjects\")}",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "      &lt;/h1&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "      &lt;p class=\"text-lg leading-7 text-gray-500 dark:text-gray-400\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "        {t(\"pages.projects.showcase\")}",

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "    &lt;/div&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "    &lt;div class=\"container py-12\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "      &lt;div class=\"-m-4 flex flex-wrap\"&gt;",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "        {",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "          projectsData.map((d) =&gt; (",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "            &lt;Card",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "              title={d.title}",

          },
          {

          },
          {

          },
          {

          }
        ]

[@layer - CSS \| MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@layer)<br/>3. [Cascade layers - Learn web development \| MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/CSS/Building_blocks/Cascade_layers)<br/>4. [Cascade layers are coming to your browser  \|  Blog  \|  Chrome for Developers](https://developer.chrome.com/blog/cascade-layers)<br/><br/># CSS @layer Cascade Layers vs !important Specificity<br/><br/>## How Cascade Layers Work<br/><br/>CSS cascade layers (@layer) provide explicit control over style priority independent of selector specificity.[1] They allow developers to organize CSS into contained layers where layer precedence overrides specificity—meaning a low-specificity rule in a higher-priority layer beats a high-specificity rule in a lower-priority layer.[5]<br/><br/>## Layer Priority Order<br/><br/>Layers are prioritized by declaration order: the first declared layer has the lowest priority, and the last declared layer has the highest priority.[2] Un-layered styles (those outside any @layer) have the highest priority of all.[1]<br/><br/>## The !important Reversal<br/><br/>The cascade changes when `!important` is used. For normal (non-important) declarations, later layers win. However, with `!important` declarations, the priority reverses: the first declared layer with !important has the highest priority, and the last has the lowest.[2] Within author styles specifically, all important declarations within CSS layers take precedence over important declarations outside layers.[2]<br/><br/>## Use Cases<br/><br/>Cascade layers are designed to replace specificity hacks and `!important` declarations, particularly when:[3]<br/>- Working with CSS from multiple sources<br/>- Managing conflicting selectors and competing specificities<br/>- Dealing with third-party styles and design systems<br/>- Avoiding the need for `!important` escalation<br/><br/>## Browser Support<br/><br/>Cascade layers have been widely available since March 2022 across Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and other browsers.[2] |

{
  "url": "https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@layer",
  "markdown": "@layer - CSS | MDN\n\n- Skip to search\n- Skip to main content\n\n# @layer\n\nBaseline Widely available\n\nThis feature is well established and works across many devices and browser versions. It’s been available across browsers since March 2022.\n\n- [Report feedback](https://survey.alchemer.com/s3/7634825/MDN-baseline-feedback?page=%2Fen-US%2Fdocs%2FWeb%2FCSS%2FReference%2FAt-rules%2F%40layer&amp;amp;level=high)\n- See full compatibility\n- [Learn more](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Baseline/Compatibility)\n\nThe&#96;@layer&#96; [CSS](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS) [at-rule](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Guides/Syntax/At-rules) is used to declare a cascade layer and can also be used to define the order of precedence in case of multiple cascade layers.\n\n## Try it\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@layer module, state;\n\n@layer state {\n  .alert {\n    background-color: brown;\n  }\n  p {\n    border: medium solid limegreen;\n  }\n}\n\n@layer module {\n  .alert {\n    border: medium solid violet;\n    background-color: yellow;\n    color: white;\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n&lt;p class=\"alert\"&gt;Beware of the zombies&lt;/p&gt;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Syntax\n\ncss\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/* statement at-rules */\n@layer layer-name;\n@layer layer-name, layer-name, layer-name;\n\n/* block at-rules */\n@layer {rules}\n@layer layer-name {rules}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nwhere:\n\nlayer-name\n\nIs the name of the cascade layer.\n\nrules\n\nIs the set of CSS rules in the cascade layer.\n\n## Description\n\nRules within a cascade layer cascade together, giving more control over the cascade to web developers. Styles that are not defined in a layer always override styles declared in named and anonymous layers.\n\nThe following diagram shows layer priorities where layers are declared in 1, 2, ..., N order.\n\nAs noted in the above diagram, important declarations, declarations with the&#96;!important&#96; flag, have priority over normal declarations, or regular declarations without the&#96;!important&#96; flag. The order of precedence among important rules is the inverse of normal rules. Transitions have the greatest precedence. Next in order of highest to lowest priority are the important [user agent](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/User_agent) declarations, important user declarations, and important author declarations; in that order. Users can specify styles using browser preferences, operating system preferences, or browser extensions. Their important declarations take precedence over author, or web developer written, important declarations.\n\nWithin author styles, all important declarations within CSS layers take precedence over any important declarations declared outside of a layer, while all normal declarations within CSS layers have a lower priority than declarations declared outside of a layer. The declaration order matters. The first declared layer gets the lowest priority and the last declared layer gets the highest priority. However, the priority is reversed when the [!important](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Values/important) flag is used.\n\nThe&#96;@layer&#96; at-rule is used to create a cascade layer in one of three ways.\n\nThe first way is to use a&#96;@layer&#96; block at-rule to create a named cascade layer with the CSS rules for that layer inside, like so:\n\ncss\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@layer utilities {\n  .padding-sm {\n    padding: 0.5rem;\n  }\n\n  .padding-lg {\n    padding: 0.8rem;\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe second way is to use a&#96;@layer&#96; statement at-rule to create one or more comma-separated named cascade layers without assigning any styles. This can be a single layer, as shown below:\n\ncss\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@layer utilities;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nMultiple layers can be defined at once, as shown below:\n\ncss\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@layer theme, layout, utilities;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis is useful because the initial order in which layers are declared indicates which layer has precedence. As with declarations, the last layer to be listed will win if declarations are found in multiple layers. Therefore, with the preceding example, if a competing rule was found in&#96;theme&#96; and&#96;utilities&#96;, the one in&#96;utilities&#96; would win and be applied.\n\nA rule in&#96;utilities&#96; would be applied even if it has lower specificity than the rule in&#96;theme&#96;. This is because once the layer order has been established, specificity and order of appearance are ignored. This enables using simpler CSS selectors because you do not have to ensure that a selector will have high enough specificity to override competing rules; all you need to ensure is that it appears in a later layer.\n\nNote: Having declared your layer names, thus setting their order, you can add CSS rules to the layer by re-declaring the name. The styles are then appended to the layer and the layer order will not be changed.\n\nThe third way is to create an unnamed layer using a&#96;@layer&#96; block at-rule without including a layer name. For example:\n\ncss\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@layer {\n  p {\n    margin-block: 1rem;\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis creates an anonymous cascade layer. This layer functions in the same way as named layers; however, rules cannot be assigned to it later. The order of precedence for anonymous layers is the order in which layers are declared, named or not, and lower than the styles declared outside of a layer.\n\nAnother way to create a cascade layer is by using [@import](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/At-rules/@import). In this case, the rules would be in the imported stylesheet. Remember that the&#96;@import&#96; at-rule must precede all other types of rules, except&#96;@charset&#96; and&#96;@layer&#96; statements (not&#96;@layer&#96; blocks).\n\ncss\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@import \"theme.css\" layer(utilities);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Nesting layers\n\nLayers may be nested. For example:\n\ncss\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@layer framework {\n  @layer layout {\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nTo append rules to the&#96;layout&#96; layer inside&#96;framework&#96;, join the two names with a&#96;.&#96;.\n\ncss\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@layer framework.layout {\n  p {\n    margin-block: 1rem;\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Formal syntax\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@layer =   @layer &lt;layer-name&gt;? { &lt;rule-list&gt; }  |  @layer &lt;layer-name&gt;# ;                \"&lt;layer-name&gt; =   &lt;ident&gt;[ '.' &lt;ident&gt;]*\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Examples\n\n### Basic example\n\nIn the following example, two CSS rules are created. One for the [](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/p) element outside of any layer and one inside a layer named&#96;type&#96; for&#96;.box p&#96;.\n\nWithout layers, the selector&#96;.box p&#96; would have the highest specificity, and therefore, the text&#96;Hello, world!&#96; will display in green. As the&#96;type&#96; layer comes before the anonymous layer created to hold non-layer content, the text will be purple.\n\nAlso notice the order. Even though we declare the non-layered style first, it's still applied after the layer styles.\n\n#### HTML\n\nhtml\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n&lt;div class=\"box\"&gt;\n  &lt;p&gt;Hello, world!&lt;/p&gt;\n&lt;/div&gt;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### CSS\n\ncss\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\np {\n  color: rebeccapurple;\n}\n\n@layer type {\n  .box p {\n    font-weight: bold;\n    font-size: 1.3em;\n    color: green;\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### Result\n\n### Assigning rules to existing layers\n\nIn the following example, two layers are created with no rules applied, then CSS rules are applied to the two layers. The&#96;base&#96; layer defines a&#96;color&#96;,&#96;border&#96;,&#96;font-size&#96;, and&#96;padding&#96;. The&#96;special&#96; layer defines a different color. As&#96;special&#96; comes last when the layers were defined, the color it provides is used and the text is displayed using&#96;rebeccapurple&#96;. All of the other rules from&#96;base&#96; still apply.\n\n#### HTML\n\nhtml\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n&lt;div class=\"item\"&gt;\n  I am displayed in &lt;code&gt;color: rebeccapurple&lt;/code&gt; because the\n  &lt;code&gt;special&lt;/code&gt; layer comes after the &lt;code&gt;base&lt;/code&gt; layer. My green\n  border, font-size, and padding come from the &lt;code&gt;base&lt;/code&gt; layer.\n&lt;/div&gt;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### CSS\n\ncss\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n@layer base, special;\n\n@layer special {\n  .item {\n    color: rebeccapurple;\n  }\n}\n\n@layer base {\n  .item {\n    color: green;\n    border: 5px solid green;\n    font-size: 1.3em;\n    padding: 0.5em;\n  }\n}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### Result\n\n## Specifications\n\n| Specification |\n| --- |\n| [CSS Cascading and Inheritance Level 5# layering](https://drafts.csswg.org/css-cascade-5/#layering) |\n\n## Browser compatibility\n\n## See also\n\n- [The future of CSS: Cascade layers](https://www.bram.us/2021/09/15/the-future-of-css-cascade-layers-css-at-layer/) on bram.us (2021)\n- [Learn: Cascade layers](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn_web_development/Core/Styling_basics/Cascade_layers)\n- [Learn: Handling conflicts](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn_web_development/Core/Styling_basics/Handling_conflicts)\n- [Introducing the CSS cascade](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Guides/Cascade/Introduction)\n- [revert-layer](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Values/revert-layer)\n- [!important](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Values/important)\n- [CSSLayerStatementRule](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CSSLayerStatementRule)\n- [CSSLayerBlockRule](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CSSLayerBlockRule)\n- [@import](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/At-rules/@import)"
}
```

Parameters:

```json
{
  "plan": "# CSS @layer Lab Demo\n\n## Overview\n\nAdd a new lab entry that teaches &#96;@layer&#96; (Cascade Layers) and contrasts it with the traditional &#96;!important&#96; approach. The lab will mirror the structure of [interpolate-size.mdx](src/content/labs/interpolate-size.mdx): frontmatter, header, explanatory content, and a side-by-side interactive demo.\n\n## Key Concepts to Cover\n\n**@layer basics:**\n- Cascade layers let you control style priority independent of selector specificity\n- Layer order: first declared = lowest priority, last declared = highest priority\n- Un-layered styles always override layered styles\n- Rules in a later layer win over earlier layers, regardless of specificity\n\n**!important vs @layer:**\n- &#96;!important&#96; creates an \"override war\"—hard to override again, leads to specificity escalation\n- With &#96;@layer&#96;, you define layer order once; later layers win without &#96;!important&#96;\n- &#96;!important&#96; reverses layer priority: first layer with &#96;!important&#96; wins (useful for allowing base layers to \"win back\" when needed)\n- &#96;@layer&#96; is the modern, maintainable alternative for third-party CSS, design systems, and utilities\n\n## Files to Create\n\n### 1. [src/content/labs/css-layer.mdx](src/content/labs/css-layer.mdx)\n\nStructure (aligned with interpolate-size):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;yaml\n---\ntitle: \"CSS @layer\"\ndescription: \"Cascade layers and why they replace !important for controlling style precedence.\"\nbadge: \"CSS\"\n---\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- **Header**: Title, short description, optional browser support notice (layers supported since March 2022)\n- **Explanatory section**: 2–3 paragraphs explaining:\n  - What &#96;@layer&#96; is and why it exists\n  - The problem with &#96;!important&#96; (override wars, maintainability)\n  - How layers solve this (explicit precedence, no specificity hacks)\n- **Interactive demo**: Two columns using a new &#96;LayerDemo&#96; component (see below)\n- **Code examples**: Inline &#96;&lt;pre&gt;&#96; blocks showing:\n  - The old way: &#96;!important&#96; escalation\n  - The new way: &#96;@layer base, utilities;&#96; and rules in each layer\n\n### 2. [src/components/labs/LayerDemo.tsx](src/components/labs/LayerDemo.tsx)\n\nNew Solid.js component (similar pattern to [ChatDemo.tsx](src/components/labs/ChatDemo.tsx)):\n\n- **Props**: &#96;useLayers?: boolean&#96; — &#96;false&#96; = &#96;!important&#96; approach, &#96;true&#96; = &#96;@layer&#96; approach\n- **Layout**: Card with border, rounded corners, dark mode support (reuse Tailwind classes from ChatDemo)\n- **Content**:\n  - **Left card (useLayers=false)**: Simulated scenario where base styles are overridden with &#96;!important&#96;. Show the resulting CSS and a small visual (e.g., a styled box). Explain the problem: \"To override this again, you need another !important.\"\n  - **Right card (useLayers=true)**: Same visual result using &#96;@layer base, utilities&#96; — base in one layer, override in another. Explain: \"Layer order controls precedence; no !important needed.\"\n- **Visual**: Simple box/button that changes background or padding based on the \"override\" to make the concept tangible\n\n### 3. Register component in [src/pages/l/[...slug].astro](src/pages/l/[...slug].astro)\n\nAdd &#96;LayerDemo&#96; to the &#96;components&#96; object passed to MDX:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;astro\nconst { Content } = await render(entry);\n---\n&lt;LabLayout entry={entry}&gt;\n  &lt;Content components={{ ChatDemo, LayerDemo }} /&gt;\n&lt;/LabLayout&gt;\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Design System Alignment\n\n- Reuse existing patterns from [interpolate-size.mdx](src/content/labs/interpolate-size.mdx) and [ChatDemo.tsx](src/components/labs/ChatDemo.tsx):\n  - Same card styling: &#96;border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700 rounded-2xl p-5 bg-white dark:bg-gray-800 shadow-sm&#96;\n  - Same badge styling: &#96;bg-emerald-100 text-emerald-800&#96; / &#96;bg-rose-100 text-rose-800&#96; for state indicators\n  - Same info box: &#96;bg-amber-50 border border-amber-200&#96; for browser support\n  - Same code block: &#96;bg-gray-800 dark:bg-gray-900 text-gray-100 font-mono text-xs&#96;\n- Use Tailwind classes from [global.css](src/styles/global.css) and existing components; no new design tokens\n\n## Demo Scenario (Concrete Example)\n\n**Scenario**: A design system defines &#96;.card { background: #e0e7ff; padding: 1rem; }&#96;. We want a variant &#96;.card--danger { background: #fecaca; }&#96;.\n\n**!important approach:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;css\n.card { background: #e0e7ff !important; }  /* Escalation begins */\n.card--danger { background: #fecaca !important; }  /* Override war */\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**@layer approach:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;css\n@layer base, overrides;\n@layer base { .card { background: #e0e7ff; padding: 1rem; } }\n@layer overrides { .card--danger { background: #fecaca; } }\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe component will render two small card-like boxes side by side, each showing the same final appearance but with different underlying CSS mechanisms and explanatory text.\n\n## Routing\n\nNo routing changes needed. The lab will be available at &#96;/l/css-layer&#96; automatically via the existing [getStaticPaths](src/pages/l/[...slug].astro) that maps &#96;lab.id&#96; to the slug.\n\n## Mermaid: Cascade Order (for plan clarity)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    subgraph normal [Normal declarations]\n        L1[Layer 1 - base] --&gt; L2[Layer 2 - components]\n        L2 --&gt; L3[Layer 3 - utilities]\n        L3 --&gt; Unlayered[Un-layered styles win]\n    end\n    subgraph important [\"!important declarations - reversed\"]\n        UnlayeredI[Un-layered] --&gt; L3I[Layer 3]\n        L3I --&gt; L2I[Layer 2]\n        L2I --&gt; L1I[Layer 1 wins]\n    end\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n",
  "name": "CSS @layer Lab Demo",
  "overview": "Create a new lab demo at &#96;src/content/labs/css-layer.mdx&#96; that explains the &#96;@layer&#96; CSS feature and demonstrates the difference between using &#96;!important&#96; and cascade layers for controlling style precedence, following the structure and patterns of the existing interpolate-size lab."
}

Summary of what was implemented:

## Summary

### 1. [src/components/labs/LayerDemo.tsx](src/components/labs/LayerDemo.tsx)
- Solid.js component with `useLayers` prop
- **Left card** (`useLayers={false}`): shows the `!important` approach with an "Override war" badge and explanation of specificity escalation
- **Right card** (`useLayers={true}`): shows the `@layer` approach with a "Maintainable" badge and explanation of layer-based precedence
- Reuses ChatDemo styling (borders, badges, code blocks)

### 2. [src/content/labs/css-layer.mdx](src/content/labs/css-layer.mdx)
- Frontmatter: title, description, badge
- Header with title, description, and browser support note (March 2022+)
- Explanatory sections:
  - What `@layer` is and how it works
  - `!important` vs `@layer` (override wars vs layer order)
  - Note on how `!important` reverses layer priority
- Side-by-side demo using `LayerDemo`
- Scoped styles for both demos: regular CSS for the `!important` card, `@layer` for the layer-based card

### 3. [src/pages/l/[...slug].astro](src/pages/l/[...slug].astro)
- Imported `LayerDemo` and passed it to MDX via `components={{ ChatDemo, LayerDemo }}`

The lab is available at **`/l/css-layer`** and appears on the labs index at `/labs`.

[Recipes for Detecting Support for CSS At-Rules \| CSS-Tricks](https://css-tricks.com/recipes-for-detecting-support-for-css-at-rules/)<br/>4. [The Future of CSS: Detect at-rule support with  @supports at-rule(@keyword)](https://www.bram.us/2022/01/20/detect-at-rule-support-with-the-at-rule-function/)<br/><br/># @supports and @layer CSS Browser Support<br/><br/>## @supports At-Rule<br/>The `@supports` at-rule has **widespread browser support** and is considered "Baseline Widely available."[1] It's been available across browsers since September 2015.[1] This feature query allows you to test browser support for CSS properties, values, selectors, and font technologies.[1]<br/><br/>The `@supports` rule can check:[1]<br/>- Declaration syntax: `@supports (property: value)`<br/>- Selector syntax: `@supports selector(h2 > p)`<br/>- Font technologies: `@supports font-tech(color-COLRv1)`<br/><br/>## @layer At-Rule<br/>The `@layer` at-rule has **more limited browser support**:[2]<br/>- **Supported:** Chrome 99+, Firefox 97+, Safari 15.4+, Edge 99+, Opera 85+<br/>- **Not supported:** Internet Explorer, older versions of major browsers<br/><br/>Mobile support includes Chrome for Android 145+, Firefox for Android 147+, and Samsung Internet 18.0+.[2]<br/><br/>## Detecting At-Rule Support<br/>Currently, there's **no direct way** to use `@supports` to test for `@layer` support itself.[3][5] The CSS Working Group decided in 2022 to add an `at-rule()` function for this purpose, but it hasn't been implemented in browsers yet.[3][5] As a workaround, developers can test related properties that shipped with the at-rule or write styles directly inside the `@layer` query, which will be ignored in unsupporting browsers.[3] |

</details>
